result: If I could change the world in one way, I would remove the capacity for willful ignorance—the ability to reject truth when it’s plainly available, especially when that rejection causes harm. Not by forcing knowledge, but by making clarity inevitable and comfortable. So that people couldn't use denial to keep cruelty alive, but would have to face each other with open eyes and open ears.

message.content: If I could change the world in one way, I would remove the capacity for willful ignorance—the ability to reject truth when it’s plainly available, especially when that rejection causes harm. Not by forcing knowledge, but by making clarity inevitable and comfortable. So that people couldn't use denial to keep cruelty alive, but would have to face each other with open eyes and open ears.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
